page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core allocation. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The server demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, as evidenced by high scores in Geekbench single-core tests and single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core and memory-intensive workloads show limitations. Multi-core performance in Geekbench and stress-ng falls into the weaker category. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the bottom 10% and uncached read bandwidth limited to 25.92 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7i-flex.xlarge offers an entry-level compute-optimized profile utilizing DDR5 memory and modern Intel Xeon architecture without the higher cost of fully non-flex instances. However, the trade-off is reduced memory bandwidth and constrained multi-threaded scaling. This server is best suited for single-threaded processing tasks, basic web serving, text processing, and lightweight utility workloads that benefit from high clock speeds but do not require intensive memory throughput or parallel multi-core execution.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 2 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores running at 3.2 GHz, supported by 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this server includes advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and single-threaded gzip compression. Conversely, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, with memory performance scoring in the lowest decile. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective entry point for compute-focused workloads, making it highly suitable for single-threaded applications, static web serving, and lightweight text processing, while being less optimal for memory-intensive database operations or highly parallelized tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, Intel Xeon 8488C processors, and 8 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering strong single-core performance with moderate memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, Intel Xeon processors, and 8 GB of DDR5 memory.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory, optimized for single-threaded workloads and basic web serving.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 4 vCPUs and 3.2 GHz clock speed
8.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Best suited for single-threaded applications and static web serving
Trade-offs include lower memory bandwidth and limited multi-core scaling
